summary: Phase E — Reference Asset Injection backend verified end-to-end against the live preview backend. 13/13 new API tests in /app/backend/tests/test_phase_e_api.py pass + 12/12 existing unit tests in test_phase_e_scene_references.py still green. Verified: (1) GET /api/admin/orders/{id}/storyboard now exposes per-scene references object with all 11 expected keys (available, child_used, extra_ids_used, extra_indexes_used, toy_used, injected_count, attempted, used, fallback_path, fallback_reason, skipped_reasons) for every scene of the legacy delivered order 4c357bfc, all returning the documented defaults (false/0/empty/null) — confirms backward compat; aggregate fields output_summary.references_total_injected, references_used_scene_count, references_skipped_total are present and equal to 0 on legacy. (2) Legacy storyboard returns HTTP 200 (no 500) and ref order 61c44457 storyboard also 200 with aggregate fields present. (3) POST /api/admin/lab/run stage_key=scene_image_generation + inputs={order_id=61c44457, scene_index=1} → status='preview-only', error_message=null, output_preview.reference_dry_run present with available.child=true, child_ref.name='محمود', injected_count=1, prompt_augmentation contains 'CHILD reference', no provider call. (4) Same lab call WITHOUT order_id → still preview-only success, output_preview.rendered_prompt_preview present, reference_dry_run absent, no error. (5) POST /api/admin/orders/4c357bfc.../pricing/snapshot?kind=actual → breakdown.items does NOT contain 'scene_reference_injection' line item (correct rule for legacy orders without scene_reference_log.references_used=true); GET /api/admin/orders/{id}/pricing also confirms the persisted snapshot omits the line item. (6) Regression: /api/admin/lab/stages still returns exactly 7 stages including scene_image_generation; storyboard still emits all 9 stages; narration_generation, video_generation, music_generation lab dry-runs still succeed as preview-only with no reference_dry_run leak.
critical_code_review_comments: scene_reference_service.py imports `from db import db` at module top, which forces MONGO_URL to be set at import time. This makes the unit tests fail to collect under pytest unless backend/.env is sourced first. Defer the import into the loader functions (or use a lazy db getter) so the pure-logic unit tests remain runnable from /app root without env setup — important for CI.

The aggregate fields references_total_injected / references_used_scene_count / references_skipped_total are emitted on output_summary even when the stage status is 'pending' (verified on order 61c44457 which has no completed scenes). Values are 0 which is correct, but consider documenting that these are best-effort counters that include in-flight scenes, otherwise admins may interpret zero as 'no references attempted yet' vs 'attempted but trimmed to zero'. A small `references_attempt_count` distinct from `references_used_scene_count` would disambiguate.

scene_reference_log is written per-scene by the orchestrator, but the 'fallback_path' / 'fallback_reason' fields are surfaced only in the response, never checked against image_generation_service.generate_image's provider_meta on the read path. If an orchestrator bug ever forgets to copy provider_meta into scene_reference_log, the storyboard will silently report fallback_path=null even when a fallback actually occurred. A storyboard-level cross-check (scene.image_generation.fallback_used vs references.fallback_path) would catch this.

Pricing rule 'line item appears only when at least one scene has scene_reference_log.references_used=true with references_injected_count>0' is implemented correctly, but the test surface for the *positive* case (line item DOES appear) cannot be exercised here without burning the Nano Banana budget. Recommend a tiny seeded fixture-order with a hand-crafted scene_plans.scene_reference_log having references_used=true so future regression tests can assert the inclusion path.

Lab dry-run reference_dry_run includes 'prompt_augmentation' as a fully-rendered string, but the actual scene_image_generation request also passes support_true_refs=True. The dry-run does not surface support_true_refs — consider adding that flag to the dry-run output so admins can preview exactly what gets sent.

Hard caps MAX_EXTRA_REFS=2 and MAX_TOY_REFS=1 are constants in scene_reference_service.py. They are not configurable via /api/admin/pipeline-config or any admin setting. If ops needs to tune these (e.g. raise extras to 3 for VIP orders), code change + redeploy is required. Consider lifting them into pipeline_config.scene_image_generation.
test_credentials: admin@gheras.com / Admin@1234. Test orders: 61c44457-2a80-454d-b3bc-5d2625085aba (has child reference, scene_index=1 confirmed), 4c357bfc-3092-4eb6-8c2a-105a6662766b (legacy delivered, no scene_reference_log).
seed_data_creation: Created one actual-pricing snapshot for order 4c357bfc via POST /pricing/snapshot?kind=actual (id=7d7a7f3e-93b3-4561-9f4d-a4641343574d). This is a normal admin operation, not a hack. It correctly omits the scene_reference_injection line item. No DB seeding for orders/scenes; no media regeneration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: `cd /app/backend && set -a && . ./.env && set +a && pytest tests/test_phase_e_api.py tests/test_phase_e_scene_references.py -v` (~3s, 25 tests total). The Phase E unit-test file requires MONGO_URL in env at collection time because scene_reference_service.py eagerly imports db at module level — see code-review comment #1. The API test file uses BASE = REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (https://girasai-create.preview.emergentagent.com) and admin login admin@gheras.com/Admin@1234. Order 61c44457 is in 'pending' state for scene_image_generation so the storyboard for it has 0 generated scenes; only the aggregate fields are exercised on it. Order 4c357bfc is the workhorse delivered order — 14 scenes, all with references defaults. Positive scene_reference_injection pricing line-item case is NOT exercised because no order in the system has scene_reference_log.references_used=true yet (would require a real Nano Banana run); main agent should add a fixture-seeded order if regression coverage of the inclusion branch is needed.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. All 6 review items behave exactly as specified. The implementation is read-safe (pricing snapshot rule correctly excludes the new line item for legacy data), forward-extensible (per-scene references object always present with documented defaults), and the lab dry-run is correctly gated on order_id+scene_index without affecting the existing preview-only stages.
